steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is "the 66-kilometer-per-second collision between these meteoroids and atmospheric gases." The head noun is collision — singular, regardless of the modifiers around it.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: the verb agrees with the head noun of the subject, not with nearby plural nouns in prepositional phrases.
  • Choice A — "produces": singular simple present; correctly agrees with singular head noun "collision." ✓
  • Choice B — "are producing": plural progressive; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "produce": plural simple present; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "have produced": plural present perfect; does not agree with singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
